facts.text: Bella is a 7-year-old female spayed Persian cat weighing 4.2 kg
Chief complaint: Polyuria and polydipsia for the past 3 weeks
Owner reports increased thirst, drinking entire water bowl daily
Litter box clumps are significantly larger, urinating 4-5 times daily
Weight loss of approximately 0.5 kg over the past 2 months
Appetite remains good to increased
No vomiting or diarrhea reported
Indoor cat, no known toxin exposure
Last veterinary visit 8 months ago, vaccinations up to date
Temperature: 38.6°C (101.5°F), Heart Rate: 200 bpm, Respiratory Rate: 32/min
Body Condition Score: 4/9 (underweight)
Physical exam: Thin, poor coat quality, palpable thyroid nodule on left side
Mild dehydration (estimated 5%), skin tent slightly delayed
Kidneys palpably small and irregular
Mucous membranes pale pink, CRT <2 seconds
Heart auscultation: Tachycardia, possible gallop rhythm
No abdominal pain on palpation
Dental tartar grade 2/4, no oral masses
Previous medical history: None significant
Current medications: None
Diet: Dry food (Science Diet Adult), free-fed
Owner noted increased vocalization and restlessness at night
No recent travel or boarding

vetNotes.diagnosis: R/O: Hyperthyroidism (most likely given palpable thyroid nodule, tachycardia, weight loss), Diabetes mellitus, Chronic kidney disease
vetNotes.treatmentPlan: Diagnostic workup: CBC, chemistry panel, urinalysis, T4 level. Recheck in 3 days with results. Consider thyroid scintigraphy if T4 elevated. Discuss treatment options based on lab results.

soapNote.content: S: 7yo FS Persian presented for PU/PD x 3 weeks...

factReconciliation.summary: Initial encounter, no prior facts to reconcile
